zh.Sydenham Damerel, Tavistock, West Devon: 英國英格蘭戴文希登哈姆 達莫勒爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Boyleston於1115年出生在英國戴文希登哈姆 達莫勒爾時，她的父親Thomas de Venator de Boyleston32歲，母親Mrs Thomas de Venator de Boyleston26歲。她於1136年與Walter de Sydenham在英國英格蘭戴文希登哈姆 達莫勒爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾德雷可特。

nameMeanings.name: Boyleston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Boylestone (Derbyshire). The placename is apparently from an Old English placename Bog-hyll ‘bow hill’, i.e. rounded hill, in the genitive case with -es, + tūn ‘farm, village’. This surname is now rare in Britain.
History: Thomas Boylston came to Watertown, MA, in 1635. His grandson Zabdiel Boylston (c. 1679–1766) introduced inoculation against smallpox to North America.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Boyleston was born in 1115, in Sydenham Damerel, Devon, England, her father, Thomas de Venator de Boyleston, was 32 and her mother, Mrs Thomas de Venator de Boyleston, was 26. She married Walter de Sydenham in 1136, in Sydenham Damerel, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in Draycott, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Boyleston est née en 1115, à Sydenham Damerel, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Venator de Boyleston, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Mrs Thomas de Venator de Boyleston, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Walter de Sydenham en 1136, à Sydenham Damerel, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée à Draycott, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda de Boylestonが1115年にイギリスデボンシデンハム ダムレルで生まれた時，父Thomas de Venator de Boylestonは32歳，母Mrs Thomas de Venator de Boylestonは26歳であった。1136年にイギリスイギリスデボンシデンハム ダムレルでWalter de Sydenhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアドレイコットで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Boyleston(이)가 1115년 영국 데폰 시데함 다머에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Venator de Boyleston(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs Thomas de Venator de Boyleston(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1136년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 시데함 다머에서 Walter de Sydenham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 드라이컷에서 사망했다.
